Phase-29.NAME: MTProto Provisioning Core
Phase-29.Goal: Port the required KPprotoN provisioning logic into KrotPN with Postgres-backed assignment state.

NonGoals.item-1: No public MTProto runtime edge changes yet.

Steps.step-1: Add MTProto settings: base domain, proxy port, base secret, private salt, SNI prefix strategy, and rotation marker.
Steps.step-2: Create Postgres assignment model and Alembic migration for M-042 with unique user and SNI constraints.
Steps.step-3: Implement deterministic SNI generation under krotpn.xyz and collision-safe fallback policy.
Steps.step-4: Implement derived fake-TLS secret generation and tg://proxy assembly using KPprotoN-compatible test vectors.
Steps.step-5: Add idempotent issue/reissue service that stores assignment metadata and returns owner-safe proxy payload.
Steps.step-6: Add backend tests for assignment persistence, derived credential behavior, rotation warning, and migration compatibility.

Verification.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-29.xml docs/verification/V-M-042.xml docs/verification/V-M-043.xml docs/verification-index.xml; mygrace lint

VerificationMatrix.matrix-1: Assignment model, repository idempotency, uniqueness constraints, restore-safe fields, migration compatibility, and secret/link redaction.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-2: Deterministic wildcard-safe SNI, derived fake-TLS secret vectors, tg://proxy assembly, idempotent issue/reissue, and safe failure paths.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-3: Settings validation, verified-user precondition, Alembic coverage, and no regressions in existing auth/migration contracts.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-4: No MTProto proxy is issued before verified-email activation; automatic issuance remains deferred until later integration phases unless explicitly added to Phase-29 execution.

PreflightGates.gate-1: Execution may stay module-local, but release-level closure must acknowledge Phase-28 partial-local V-M-004 and V-M-022 debt until those route/API gates pass.
PreflightGates.gate-2: Automated tests must not call live Telegram, mutate host VPN state, or touch deploy/install scripts.
PreflightGates.gate-3: All logs and admin-safe responses must redact base secret, salt, derived secret, token material, and full tg://proxy links.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: One verified user maps to one canonical MTProto assignment unless explicit rotation is requested.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Derived secret differs from raw base secret and is bound to the assigned SNI.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Assignment restore from Postgres is sufficient to rebuild user-safe response payloads.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Base secret or salt rotation is represented as a reissue-required condition.

Completion.item-1: M-042 now has SQLModel assignment metadata, a repository, unique user/SNI invariants, and an Alembic revision for mtproto_assignments.
Completion.item-2: M-043 now has deterministic SNI generation, KPprotoN-compatible fake-TLS derivation, tg://proxy assembly, and idempotent verified-user issue/reissue service.
Completion.item-3: Phase-29 backend verification passed locally: 14 targeted tests across assignment registry, provisioning, and migration evidence.

RefreshNotes.note-1: Release-level closure still inherits Phase-28 partial-local debt for V-M-004 and V-M-022 route/API gates; Phase-29 itself is module-local complete.

Risks.risk-1: Wrong fake-TLS secret format will produce links Telegram rejects even if backend tests pass superficially.
Risks.risk-2: Storing full proxy links unnecessarily increases breach impact.
